page: Amazon Web Services m7i.2xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a 105 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware accelerators, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most standard workloads. The single-core Geekbench score is 1790.0, and the multi-core score is 6809.0. The processor features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in specialized vector and matrix operations. In text processing, the instance demonstrates strong performance with a Geekbench text processing score of 2359.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 579.72 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and approximately 50.51 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance processes 1017.23 tokens per second for prompt processing using a small 135M model, though text generation on a medium 7B model is relatively weak at 7.71 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory or storage-optimized hardware. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which is a tradeoff to consider for disk-heavy applications. Given its balanced CPU-to-RAM ratio, dedicated CPU allocation, and strong text processing capabilities, this server is well-suited for applications such as web serving, Redis caching, software compilation, and general-purpose business applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services m7i.2xlarge is a general-purpose server instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, alongside 32.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1790.0 and a multi-core score of 6809.0, alongside strong text processing performance. Supported by Intel AVX-512 and AMX extensions, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline, suitable for balanced business workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for stable, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Features Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
